theme.title: Direct and Indirect Objects
theme.description: Practice direct object pronouns, indirect object pronouns, and combinations of both in practical Spanish.

lesson.title: Lesson 1: Direct Objects
lesson.instructions: Practice common direct object pronouns with short everyday sentences.

exercises.prompt: I see the movie. I see it.
We buy the books. We buy them.
exercises.explanation: Use 'los' for a masculine plural direct object.
'La' is the feminine direct object pronoun.

exercises.answer: I still do not understand them.
exercises.answer_alt: I do not understand them yet.

exercises.prompt: Do you have the keys? Yes, I have them here.
exercises.answer: Tienes las llaves? Si, las tengo aqui.

exercises.prompt: My brother is reading the emails now. He is reading them now.
exercises.answer: Mi hermano esta leyendo los correos ahora. Los esta leyendo ahora.
exercises.answer_alt: Mi hermano esta leyendo los correos ahora. Esta leyendolos ahora.
exercises.explanation: The direct object pronoun can go before the conjugated verb or attach to the gerund.

exercises.answer: I want to buy it tomorrow.
exercises.explanation: The pronoun attaches to the infinitive.

exercises.prompt: Las vamos a visitar este fin de semana.
exercises.answer: We are going to visit them this weekend.
exercises.answer_alt: We are going to visit them this coming weekend.
exercises.explanation: 'Las' refers to a feminine plural object.
